segments.s710-g33: همی‌خواهم is a poetic present-tense verb meaning 'I wish/ask' (the prefix همی‑ gives a continuous or emphatic present). از marks the source 'from/of'. کردگار جهان is a genitive/construct phrase 'Creator of the world' (the ezafe is omitted in poetry), so the whole segment says 'I ask/from the Creator of the world.'
segments.s710-g34: ز is the poetic variant of از ('from'). روزی‌ده is a deverbal/compound noun 'the one who gives sustenance' (bestower/giver). آشکار و نهان are coordinated adjectives/adverbial attributes ('manifest/clear and hidden'), describing the manner of giving; together they mean 'who gives sustenance openly and secretly' or 'the giver of manifest and hidden sustenance.'
segments.s710-g35: که introduces a relative clause ('who/that') referring back to the Creator. یکسر is an adverb 'entirely/once and for all' placed before the verb for emphasis. ببخشد is third-person singular present/optative 'forgive' (in context an optative/hopeful sense: 'may he forgive' or 'who will forgive'). گناه مرا is the object 'my sin' (مرا is the object clitic split here as 'م' + 'را', with را marking the definite object); note the verb precedes the object here, a common poetic inversion.
segments.s710-g36: درخشان کند is a causative construction 'make [something] shining/bright' where کند is the auxiliary 'make/do'. تیره گاه is a noun phrase 'dark time/dark period' (تیره = dark, گاه = time/occasion). مرا again is the clitic marking the affected/definite object ('my' / 'me' as possessor or definite object), so the whole means 'and make my dark times bright.'
segments.s710-g17: همی‌بود is an archaic past-habitual/perfective copula meaning ‘was/continued to be.’ همواره is an adverb (‘always’) modifying that state. با من is a prepositional phrase ‘with me,’ and درشت is an adjective used predicatively (‘harsh, rude’). The clause means literally ‘he was always harsh with me.’
segments.s710-g18: برآشفت is a past tense verb ‘he grew angry/was enraged.’ The coordinating و links the two actions. یکباره is an adverb (‘suddenly’), and بنمود is an older past form ‘showed/turned,’ taking پشت (‘back’) as its object: ‘suddenly he showed/turned his back.’
segments.s710-g19: برفت here functions as the simple past ‘he went/left’ (subject understood from context). The coordinating و links the clause to the next; غم و رنجش is a compound noun phrase ‘sorrow and suffering/resentment,’ ایدر ‘here’ marks the location, and بماند is a verbal form meaning ‘remained/was left’ (classical usage with ب- + ماند).
segments.s710-g20: دل و دیده من is a possessive noun phrase ‘my heart and eyes’ (من as possessor placed after the nouns). به خون is a prepositional phrase ‘in/with blood,’ and درنشاند is a past transitive verb ‘he caused to be put / imbued’—together ‘he filled/imbued my heart and eyes with blood’ (a poetic metaphor).
segments.s710-g21: کنون is an adverb ‘now’ placed before the clause; او is the third-person subject ‘he.’ سوی روشنایی is a goal phrase ‘toward the light,’ and رسید is a simple past verb ‘arrived/reached.’ The order is adverb – subject – goal – verb.
segments.s710-g22: پدر را is a definite, object-marked NP literally ‘the father’ (here functioning as the noun whose place is concerned). همی is an emphatic/adverbial particle (classical usage), جای is ‘place,’ and خواهد گزید is a periphrastic future: خواهد (future/auxiliary) + گزید (past-stem of گزیدن 'to choose/take'), yielding ‘he will take/occupy (the) place.’ Word order places the marked NP before the verb complex.
segments.s710-g23: برآمد is a past verb ‘arose/occurred.’ چنین here means ‘such’ or ‘thus’ modifying روزگار (‘time/period’), and دراز is a postpositive adjective ‘long.’ The phrase expresses ‘such a long time arose/passed.’
segments.s710-g24: کزان is a relative/prepositional compound ‘from which,’ introducing the clause about companions. همرهان is the plural ‘companions,’ کس here functions in a negative sense ‘no one,’ نگشتند is a past negative verb ‘did not return/turn back’ (negated by the verb ending), and باز (‘back/again’) emphasizes return. Together: ‘from which none of the companions returned.’
segments.s710-g25: همانا is a discourse particle meaning 'truly/indeed' placed at the start for emphasis. مرا is the object 'me' (here split in the token list as م + را); چشم دارد is a compound: چشم (lit. 'eye') plus دارد ('has') = 'has an eye on' / 'keeps watch over'. همی is the older continuous/emphatic particle (like modern هم) marking habitual/continuous aspect: 'keeps on, constantly'. The clause literally reads 'Truly (someone) has an eye on me continually.'
segments.s710-g26: ز ... آمدن is a causal/agentless nominal construction: ز (here 'because of' / 'on account of') + دیر آمدن ('late coming/arrival'). خشم دارد is present-tense 'has anger / is angry'. همی again marks continuing/habitual aspect. Together: 'He is angered on account of the late coming (i.e. my being late).' The subject ('he' who watches) is understood from the previous clause.
segments.s710-g27: ورا here means 'for him' / 'as for him'. سال سی = 'thirty years (old)'. بد in older Persian is an archaic spelling of بود ('was') and functions as the past copula 'was'. مرا شصت و هفت = 'to me (or I) sixty‑seven' — a contrast of ages (he thirty, I sixty‑seven). The clause states ages in past time; word order is topical (ورا, then age, then copula).
segments.s710-g28: نپرسید is the past tense of پرسیدن with the negative prefix ن‑; here it should be read 'did not ask' (the token senses list gives only 'asked', so note the negative prefix). زین = 'about this/this saddle' (زین = saddle), پیر = 'old (man)'. The coordination 'و تنها برفت' links: و 'and' + تنها 'alone' + برفت (classical بَرفت) = 'he went off/rode away'. So: 'He did not ask about the saddle, and the old man departed alone.'
segments.s710-g29: وی = 'he' (subject), اندر شتاب = 'in haste', و = 'and', من = 'I', اندر درنگ = 'in delay/hesitation'. This is a balanced contrastive construction: 'He is hurried, and I am slow/deliberate.' Word order places the contrasted topics (وی ... من ...) at the start of each conjunct.
segments.s710-g30: ز کردارها = 'from/through the deeds (our actions)' (ز = 'from/of', کردارها = plural 'deeds'). تا چه آید = an idiomatic clause meaning 'so that / to see what will happen' (تا marks the result/purpose and introduces چه آید 'what comes'). به چنگ = 'into (one's) grasp' (به = 'into/towards', چنگ literally 'claw' used figuratively as 'grasp'). The line asks what result their deeds will yield.
segments.s710-g31: روان تو = 'your soul/spirit'. دارنده روشن = 'holder/bearer of light' (دارنده = 'bearer/holder', روشن = 'bright/light'). کناد is an archaic 3rd-person optative/subjunctive (classical form of کند) meaning 'may (it) do / may it be so'. The clause is a wish: 'May your soul be a bearer of light.'
segments.s710-g32: خرد = 'wisdom'. پیش جان تو = 'before your soul' / 'in front of your life' (پیش = 'before/in front of', جان تو = 'your soul'). جوشن = 'armor/cuirass' (metaphorical protection). کناد again is the optative 'may it make'. The clause prays that wisdom serve as protective armor for the person's soul.
segments.s710-g9: که introduces a subordinate/time clause ('that/when'). نوبت مرا بود literally 'the turn was to me' (word order: noun + clitic object مرا + بود as past copula), with مرا = 'to me' (object/possessive) and بود in past tense. بی‌کام من places the predicate adjective 'unfulfilled' before the pronoun من for emphasis: 'I was left unfulfilled.'
segments.s710-g10: چرا starts a direct question 'why'. رفتی and بردی are both 2nd-person past verbs ('you went/left' and 'you carried/took away'); و simply coordinates the two actions. آرام من is a noun + pronoun construction meaning 'my peace/rest' (pronoun after noun marks possession in poetic Persian).
segments.s710-g11: ز بدها is a poetic prepositional phrase 'from/out of the bad things' ('among evils'). تو بودی is the past-tense copula 'you were', and مرا را دستگیر (here split as م + را + دستگیر) means 'my succour/aide' — دستگیر in this context is the beneficial sense 'succouring/helpful' rather than 'seizing'. Note the clitic pronoun + را marks the object/emphasis.
segments.s710-g12: چرا begins a question 'why'. چاره جستی is a verb phrase 'you sought a remedy' (جستی = 'sought/searched for' here). ز همراه پیر uses ز = 'from' (poetic) + همراه پیر = 'the old companion' — together meaning 'why did you go to/seek help from the old companion?'.
segments.s710-g13: مگر here functions as a rhetorical/expectant particle ('did you by chance / is it that...'), introducing a surprised question. همرهان جوان is 'young companions' (compound noun: companion + adjective), with یافتی the 2nd-person past 'you found.' Word order is typical Persian: noun phrase then verb at the end.
segments.s710-g14: که introduces the subordinate clause 'that/so that'. از پیش من = 'from before me' meaning 'ahead of me' (پیش + pronoun), and تیز بشتافتی is a compound predicate: تیز 'swift' modifies the verb بشتافتی ('you hastened/ran off') in the past 2nd person.
segments.s710-g15: جوان را uses را as a topical/emphatic marker attached to 'the young man.' چو شد 'when it became' (چو = when, شد = became) introduces a temporal clause; سال برسی و هفت is a compressed archaic numeral phrasing meaning 'the years reached thirty-seven' (برسی here functions as 'reached/attained' and و هفت adds the seven). Word order places the time/age phrase before the result clause.
segments.s710-g16: نه (implied negative 'not') + بر آرزو یافت means 'he found/obtained not according to his desire' — یافت here is past 'obtained/found'. گیتی برفت is a terse, poetic way to say 'the world went away/was gone' (برفت = went off/was gone); the result clause states that his wish was not fulfilled before life/world passed.
segments.s710-g1: مرا سال بگذشت is a common classical Persian sequence meaning “a year passed for me”: مرا is the topical/affected pronoun ‘me/for me’ (here built from م + را), سال is the temporal noun ‘year’, and بگذشت is the past verb ‘passed’. برشست و پنج follows as a separate clause: برشست is a past of برنشستن (literary) ‘sat/assumed (the throne or seat)’ and و پنج is the conjunction + numeral ‘and five’, i.e. continuing the temporal/ordinal comment. Word order is topic (مرا) → time (سال) → verb (بگذشت); the next verb (برشست) resumes the narrative.
segments.s710-g2: نه نیکو بود‌گر is a short conditional clause: نه نیکو بود = ‘it was not good/right’ (نه negates نیکو), and -گر attached to بود marks the conditional sense ‘if it were’. بیازم is a 1st-person optative/subjunctive (‘that I open / that I force open’) and به گنج is the prepositional object ‘into/at the treasure’. The clause means roughly ‘it would not be good if I were to open (break into) the treasure.’
segments.s710-g3: مگر here introduces an exception/conditional sense ‘unless’. بهره بر گیرم is a verb phrase where بهره ‘benefit/share’ is fronted and بر + گیرم (‘I take up / I seize’) is the verbal core; از پند خویش is the source phrase ‘from my own counsel’ (از = from, پند = advice, خوی + ش = own/his (possessive)). The verb گیرم is present-subjunctive/optative in form, giving a tentative ‘I might take’.
segments.s710-g4: بر اندیشم is a verb phrase in which بر is the prepositional/coverb ‘upon’ and اندیشم is 1st-person present/subjunctive of ‘think’ (I think / I will consider). از مرگ فرزند خویش is the object phrase ‘about/concerning the death of my child’ (از = about/concerning, مرگ = death, فرزند = child, خوی + ش = my/own). The whole clause carries the sense ‘I will ponder/turn my thoughts to my child's death.’
segments.s710-g5: مرا بود نوبت is an inverted existential/topic expression: مرا (me/for me) + بود (was) + نوبت (turn) = ‘it was my turn’ or ‘my turn it was’. برفت آن جوان contains the past verb رفت/برفت (here written as برفت) ‘departed/went away’ followed by آن جوان ‘that young man’. The clause states that the young man left/was taken away after noting the speaker's turn.
segments.s710-g6: ز دردش = ‘from/because of his pain’ (ز is the literary form of از used to indicate cause or source, درد = pain, ش = his). منم is the emphatic present ‘I am’ (or ‘it is I’). چون تن بی‌روان is a simile: چون = like/as, تن = body, بی‌روان = without soul/spirit (lifeless), so ‘I am like a lifeless body’ (i.e., utterly bereft). The clause links the speaker’s state to the young man’s suffering.
segments.s710-g7: شتابم همی is present/imperfective 1st-singular ‘I hurry’ with the enclitic همی adding continuous/emphatic aspect (‘I am hurrying’). تا introduces purpose or result ‘so that / until’, and یابمش is the verb یابم + object suffix ش ‘that I may find him’ (subjunctive/optative sense). The clause reads ‘I hurry so that perhaps I may find him’ (مگر here has a speculative/limiting flavor).
segments.s710-g8: چو یابم (written together as چویابم) is a temporal clause ‘when/as soon as I find (him)’; به is the direction/preposition ‘to/toward’ and بیغاره is an archaic/place word (textual/lexical; here left as a nominal destination). بشتابمش is a 1st-person hortative/future-like form ‘I will hasten (to him)’ (بـ‑ شتاب‑ م‑ ش = I hasten to him). Together: ‘when I find him, I will hasten to (that place/person).’

sentences.s710-s17: I beseech the Creator of the world, the bestower of sustenance, both manifest and hidden.
sentences.s710-s18: who will wholly forgive my sin and turn my dark times to brightness.
sentences.s710-s9: He had always been harsh with me; he became angry and suddenly turned his back.
sentences.s710-s10: He departed, and sorrow and suffering remained here; he filled my heart and eyes with blood.
sentences.s710-s11: Now he has reached the light; he will take his father's place.
sentences.s710-s12: Thus a long time came about, from which none of the companions returned.
sentences.s710-s13: Truly he keeps his eye on me always, and he is angry at my coming late.
sentences.s710-s14: He was thirty years old; I was sixty‑seven. He did not ask about the saddle, and the old man went off alone.
sentences.s710-s15: He in haste and I in delay; from our deeds, let us see what comes into hand.
sentences.s710-s16: May your spirit be a bearer of light; may wisdom make a cuirass before your soul.
sentences.s710-s5: When it was my turn and I remained unfulfilled, why did you go and carry off my peace?
sentences.s710-s6: You were my succour among evils; why did you seek a remedy from that old companion?
sentences.s710-s7: Did you perhaps find young companions so that you rushed off ahead of me?
sentences.s710-s8: When the young man's years reached thirty-seven, he did not attain his desire and the world passed away.
sentences.s710-s1: A year passed for me; he took his seat for five (years) — it would not be right if I were to force open the treasure.
sentences.s710-s2: Unless I take some profit from my own counsel, I will think upon the death of my child.
sentences.s710-s3: It was my turn; that young man departed — from his pain I am like a body without a soul.
sentences.s710-s4: I hurry on so that perhaps I may find him; when I find him I will hasten toward (or to) Bi‑ghāreh.
